title: thymus serpyllum var. stenophyllus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. stenophyllus, commonly known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of essential oils. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its potential to aid digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Creeping thyme can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and topical applications, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for a range of health benefits.
